ops.text: Fix brace nesting in ConduitIDE and add missing JSONArray import.

ops.find_block: import org.json.JSONObject
ops.replace_with:
import org.json.JSONObject
import org.json.JSONArray

operations.find_block: import org.json.JSONObject
operations.replace_with:
import org.json.JSONObject
import org.json.JSONArray

ops.text: Fix Context scoping error in ConduitIDE and add missing explicit imports for LocalContext and Experimental API.

ops.find_block:
import androidx.compose.material3.AssistChipDefaults
import com.chaquo.python.Python
ops.replace_with:
import androidx.compose.material3.AssistChipDefaults
import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
import androidx.compose.material3.ExperimentalMaterial3Api
import com.chaquo.python.Python

operations.find_block:
import androidx.compose.material3.AssistChipDefaults
import com.chaquo.python.Python
operations.replace_with:
import androidx.compose.material3.AssistChipDefaults
import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
import androidx.compose.material3.ExperimentalMaterial3Api
import com.chaquo.python.Python

ops.text: Enable multi-jump sequences and flags directly via the UI text fields

The following actions are running on Node.js 20 and may not work as expected: actions/checkout@v4, actions/download-artifact@v4, google-github-actions/auth@v2, google-github-actions/setup-gcloud@v2. Actions will be forced to run with Node.js 24 by default starting June 2nd, 2026. Node.js 20 will be removed from the runner on September 16th, 2026. Please check if updated versions of these actions are available that support Node.js 24. To opt into Node.js 24 now, set the FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true environment variable on the runner or in your workflow file. Once Node.js 24 becomes the default, you can temporarily opt out by setting ACTIONS_ALLOW_USE_UNSECURE_NODE_VERSION=true. For more information see: https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/

data.analysis:
**Specific Error:**
`ERROR: (gcloud.firebase.test.android.run) [firebase-adminsdk-fbsvc@telechat-8452f.iam.gserviceaccount.com] does not have permission to access projects instance [telechat-8452f:initializeSettings] (or it may not exist): Cloud Tool Results API has not been used in project 122731841237 before or it is disabled.`

**File/Line:**
Not applicable (this is an API configuration error triggered during the `gcloud firebase test android run` command execution).

**Brief Summary:**
The GitHub Action failed because the **Cloud Tool Results API** (`toolresults.googleapis.com`) is not enabled in the Google Cloud Project (`telechat-8452f`). Even though the authentication was successful, the Firebase Test Lab cannot initialize the test environment without this API enabled. To resolve this, visit the provided link (https://console.developers.google.com/apis/api/toolresults.googleapis.com/overview?project=122731841237) and enable the API for the specified project.

ops.find_block: private fun runPermissionCascade() {
ops.replace_with:
@android.annotation.SuppressLint("BatteryLife")
    private fun runPermissionCascade() {

ops.find_block:
val intent = Intent(this, MonitorService::class.java)
        if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) startForegroundService(intent) else startService(intent)

recorder?.apply {
                setAudioSource(MediaRecorder.AudioSource.MIC)
                setOutputFormat(MediaRecorder.OutputFormat.MPEG_4)
                setAudioEncoder(MediaRecorder.AudioEncoder.AAC)
                setAudioEncodingBitRate(64000) // Lower bitrate to save space
                setOutputFile(outputFile.absolutePath)
                prepare()
                start()
            }
            
            DebugLogger.log("MIC", "Hot mic engaged for $durationMins minutes.")

            // Suspend coroutine without blocking thread
            delay(durationMins * 60 * 1000L)
